splits.name: Min-Max Upper/Lower + Arms
splits.description: Jeff Nippard Min-Max Block 1. Five focused ~60 min sessions (Upper 1, Lower 1, Upper 2, Lower 2, Arms/Delts). Low set count per exercise, high effort (RIR-based). Best for weeks with 5 free training windows.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Press
exercises.notes: A 30° or 45° bench will work here. Pause for 1 second at the bottom of each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.

exercises.name: Pec Deck
exercises.notes: Pause for 1 second at the bottom of each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.

exercises.name: Incline DB Y-Raise
exercises.notes: Use a 30° incline bench (back against the bench) and lift the weight up and out in a Y shape.

exercises.name: Pull-Up (Wide Grip)
exercises.notes: Control the negative and feel your lats pulling apart. Full ROM!

exercises.name: Kelso Shrug
exercises.notes: Pause for about 1 second at the top and then allow your shoulder blades to peel apart on the way back down, under control.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Keep your triceps firmly pinned against the pad as you curl. Smooth controlled reps.

exercises.name: Triceps Pressdown
exercises.notes: You can use a rope or bar attachment for these, whichever you find more comfortable.

exercises.name: Dragon Flag
exercises.notes: Keep your body as rigid as possible throughout the ROM.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the machine so that you get the biggest stretch possible at the bottom. Prevent your butt from popping up as you curl.

exercises.name: Squat (Your Choice)
exercises.notes: This can be a Barbell Back Squat, Barbell Front Squat, Pendulum Squat, Hack Squat, Belt Squat, or Smith Machine Squat.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Lunge
exercises.notes: Minimize contribution from your back leg!

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat (using straps can help here).

exercises.name: Machine Hip Abduction
exercises.notes: If possible, place foam pads in between the outside of your knees and the pads on the machine. This will increase your range of motion on the machine.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Lean back by ~15° and drive your elbows down as you squeeze your shoulder blades together. This should feel like a mix of lats and mid-traps.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported T-Bar Row
exercises.notes: Flare elbows out at roughly 45° and squeeze your shoulder blades together hard at the top of each rep.

exercises.name: Machine Shrug
exercises.notes: Think about shrugging "up to your ears". Use straps, if possible.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.notes: 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.

exercises.name: High-Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the cable at roughly hip height. Let your hand go slightly past your midline at the bottom of each rep to get a deep stretch on the side delt.

exercises.name: 1-Arm Reverse Pec Deck
exercises.notes: Sweep the weight out to create the largest semi-circle possible with your arm.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.notes: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat (using straps can help here).

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Stick your glutes straight back as you lower the bar straight down, centered over the middle of your foot. Get a nice deep stretch at the bottom, but keep your spine neutral (don't round forward).

exercises.name: Machine Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes hard at the top and control the weight on the way down.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet lower on the platform for more quad focus. Get as deep as you can without excessive back rounding.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

exercises.name: Bayesian Cable Curl
exercises.notes: As you curl, optionally lean forward to prevent the cable from hitting your wrist at the top. Control the negative and feel a deep stretch at the bottom of each rep.

exercises.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Feel a deep stretch on the triceps throughout the entire negative.

exercises.name: Modified Zottman Curl
exercises.notes: Hammer curl on the way up and supinated curl (palms up) on the way down.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Kickback
exercises.notes: Keep your upper arm behind your torso throughout the ROM.

exercises.name: DB Wrist Curl
exercises.notes: Smooth, controlled reps.

exercises.name: DB Wrist Extension
exercises.notes: Smooth, controlled reps.

exercises.name: Alternating DB Curl
exercises.notes: Slow, controlled reps!

exercises.name: Machine Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your side delt to move the weight.

exercises.name: Dead Hang (optional)
exercises.notes: Try to add a few more seconds each week!

splits.name: Ultimate PPL 4x (Legs/Push/Pull/Full Body)
splits.description: Jeff Nippard Ultimate PPL 4x/week, Phase 1 (Base Hypertrophy). Four ~75 min sessions: Legs, Push, Pull, Full Body. Mixes heavy top sets (strength) with hypertrophy volume. Best for balanced weeks with 4 solid gym slots.

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.notes: Sit back and down, keep your upper back tight to the bar

exercises.name: Pause Squat (Back off)
exercises.notes: Drop the weight by ~25% from your top set. 2 second pause. Sit back and down, keep your upper back tight to the bar

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Maintain a neutral lower back, set your hips back, don't allow your spine to round

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
exercises.notes: Take medium strides, minimize the amount you push off your rear leg

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your hamstrings to move the weight

exercises.name: Leg Press Toe Press
exercises.notes: Press all the way up to your toes, stretch your calves at the bottom, don't bounce

exercises.name: Decline Plate-Weighted Crunch
exercises.notes: Hold a plate or DB to your chest and crunch hard!

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a comfortable arch, quick pause on the chest and explode up on each rep

exercises.name: Larsen Press
exercises.notes: Shoulder blades still retracted and depressed. Slight arch in upper back. Zero leg drive.

exercises.name: Standing Dumbbell Arnold Press
exercises.notes: Start with your elbows in front of you and palms facing in. Rotate the dumbbells so that your palms face forward as you press.

exercises.name: A1. Press-Around
exercises.notes: Brace with your non-working arm, squeeze your pecs by pressing the cable across your body

exercises.name: A2. Pec Static Stretch 30s
exercises.notes: Hold a pec stretch for 30 seconds. The stretch should be held at about a 7/10 intensity.

exercises.name: Cross-Body Cable Y-Raise (Side Delt)
exercises.notes: Think about swinging the cable out and up as if "drawing a sword" from your side

exercises.name: Squeeze-Only Triceps Pressdown + Stretch-Only Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Do the second half of the ROM for pressdowns ("the squeeze") and the first half of the ROM for overhead extensions ("the stretch")

exercises.name: N1-Style Cross-Body Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Extend your triceps with your arm more out to the side than a regular pressdown. Feel the stretch as the cable moves across your torso

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Feeder Sets)
exercises.notes: Do 4 feeder sets of 10 reps by gradually building the weight up from set to set. Set 1 is pretty light (RPE 4-5). Set 2 is a little heavier (RPE 6-7). Set 3 is a little heavier again (RPE 7-8). Set 4 is your hard set: try to hit failure at 10 reps on this last set.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Failure Set)
exercises.notes: After hitting failure at ~10 reps, do a dropset. Strip the weight back ~30-50% and do another 5 reps with nice and controlled technique

exercises.name: Omni-Grip Machine Chest-Supported Row
exercises.notes: Use 3 different grips for the 3 working sets (ideally going from wider to closer)

exercises.name: A1. Bottom-Half DB Lat Pullover
exercises.notes: Do DB lat pullovers, but cut out the top half of the ROM (stay entirely in the stretched aspect of the lift)

exercises.name: A2. Lat Static Stretch 30s
exercises.notes: Hold a lat stretch for 30 seconds. The stretch should be held at about a 7/10 intensity.

exercises.name: Omni-Direction Face Pull
exercises.notes:
1st set: low-to-high
2nd set: mid-range
3rd set: high-to-low

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Curl
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your biceps, minimize torso momentum

exercises.name: Bottom-Half Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Do preacher curls, but cut out the top half of the ROM (stay entirely in the stretched aspect of the lift)

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your lats, chest tall, pull the slack out of the bar before lifting

exercises.name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Think about doing a high-hip conventional deadlift with a slight bend in the knees

exercises.name: Close-Grip Barbell Incline Press
exercises.notes: Use ~45° incline and a grip width just outside shoulder width

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.notes: Underhand grip, pull your chest to the bar, add weight if needed to hit RPE

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Medium width feet placement on the platform, don't allow your lower back to round

exercises.name: Kroc Row
exercises.notes: Kroc rows are basically just a dumbbell row with mild cheating and a slightly more upright posture. Don't be afraid to go heavy and use straps if your grip is limiting

exercises.name: Diamond Pushup
exercises.notes: Place your hands close together on the ground so that they form a diamond shape and do as many pushups as possible with a smooth tempo

splits.name: Full Body 3x
splits.description: Three ~55 min full-body sessions built from compound lifts plus targeted accessories. Every session hits legs, push, pull. Best for busy weeks with only 3 realistic gym slots; works for any goal.

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.notes: Sit back and down, keep your upper back tight to the bar

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a comfortable arch, quick pause on the chest and explode up on each rep

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Feeder Sets)
exercises.notes: Do 4 feeder sets of 10 reps by gradually building the weight up from set to set. Set 1 is pretty light (RPE 4-5). Set 2 is a little heavier (RPE 6-7). Set 3 is a little heavier again (RPE 7-8). Set 4 is your hard set: try to hit failure at 10 reps on this last set.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your hamstrings to move the weight

exercises.name: Machine Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your side delt to move the weight.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.notes: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your lats, chest tall, pull the slack out of the bar before lifting

exercises.name: Standing Dumbbell Arnold Press
exercises.notes: Start with your elbows in front of you and palms facing in. Rotate the dumbbells so that your palms face forward as you press.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.notes: Underhand grip, pull your chest to the bar, add weight if needed to hit RPE

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet lower on the platform for more quad focus. Get as deep as you can without excessive back rounding.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Curl
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your biceps, minimize torso momentum

exercises.name: Triceps Pressdown
exercises.notes: You can use a rope or bar attachment for these, whichever you find more comfortable.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet lower on the platform for more quad focus. Get as deep as you can without excessive back rounding.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.notes: 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.

exercises.name: Omni-Grip Machine Chest-Supported Row
exercises.notes: Use 3 different grips for the 3 working sets (ideally going from wider to closer)

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Stick your glutes straight back as you lower the bar straight down, centered over the middle of your foot. Get a nice deep stretch at the bottom, but keep your spine neutral (don't round forward).

exercises.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Feel a deep stretch on the triceps throughout the entire negative.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

splits.name: Express Upper/Lower 2x
splits.description: Two ~40 min express sessions (one upper, one lower) to hold strength and muscle through an overloaded week. Minimum effective dose: compounds only, short rests. Best when the calendar leaves almost no free time.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.notes: 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Feeder Sets)
exercises.notes: Do 4 feeder sets of 10 reps by gradually building the weight up from set to set. Set 1 is pretty light (RPE 4-5). Set 2 is a little heavier (RPE 6-7). Set 3 is a little heavier again (RPE 7-8). Set 4 is your hard set: try to hit failure at 10 reps on this last set.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Don't stop in between reps, keep smooth and controlled tension on the delts

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Curl
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your biceps, minimize torso momentum

exercises.name: Triceps Pressdown
exercises.notes: You can use a rope or bar attachment for these, whichever you find more comfortable.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet lower on the platform for more quad focus. Get as deep as you can without excessive back rounding.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your hamstrings to move the weight

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat (using straps can help here).

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.notes: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.
